title: lacinaria scariosa var. squarrulosa
intro: Lacinaria scariosa var. squarrulosa, also known as Squarrose Lacinaria or Squarrose Monk's Hood, is a subspecies of the Lacinaria scariosa plant, belonging to the Bellflower family (Campanulaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Lacinaria scariosa var. squarrulosa include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its potential to soothe skin irritations. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and skin conditions.
